lessons: Lesson 15 — Cash Floor Priority in Rate Rotation: When portfolio cash drops below the 20% minimum floor during a macro selloff, restoring the cash floor takes absolute precedence over pursuing new opportunities — even compelling ones. lessons: Lesson 19 — Borderline ADX Override Zone (35-39): When MACD turns bearish but ADX is 35-39 (approaching but not yet at the Lesson 2 threshold of 40) with OBV rising, treat as a borderline HOLD — not a full override and not an exit signal. ADX must confirm above 40 in the following week to maintain the MACD-bearish override. Practical protocol: hold the week of the signal, then the following week check (a) did ADX cross above 40? If yes, Lesson 2 override applies fully; (b) is OBV still rising? If both (a) and (b) fail, the MACD bearish flip is now actionable. Trigger: MACD bearish + ADX 35-39 + OBV rising = hold one week and watch; MACD bearish + ADX stays below 40 + OBV turns flat/negative = reduce or exit.

Lesson 20 — Post-Earnings Re-entry on Stopped-Out Names: When a previously stopped-out position reports a strong earnings beat that drives a meaningful price recovery, treat this as a valid fresh entry — not revenge trading or chasing. Key distinctions: (1) the earnings beat provides fundamental validation that the original thesis was directionally correct even though the technical stop was correctly triggered; (2) the re-entry price is substantially better than the original entry (improving risk/reward); (3) MACD must be bullish and ADX must be building at re-entry. The stop-out was the correct mechanical decision; the re-entry is a new trade with a new catalyst. Trigger: Previous stop-out + strong earnings beat with positive price reaction + MACD bullish + ADX > 25 at re-entry = valid new long. Distinguish from chasing: if re-entry price is above original entry price, require ADX > 35 to justify.

response.world_assessment: Russia has launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022 — the most significant geopolitical event in Europe since World War II. Ground troops, airstrikes, and naval operations are active across multiple fronts. The market implications are unambiguous and directional: (1) Energy prices surge — Russia produces ~10M barrels/day of oil and supplies ~40% of Europe's natural gas via pipeline; Western sanctions will constrict supply into an already tight market; oil above $100/barrel is now highly probable; (2) LNG becomes strategically critical — Europe must immediately diversify away from Russian gas, and US LNG is the primary alternative; (3) Cybersecurity demand surges acutely — state-sponsored Russian cyber attacks on Western critical infrastructure are the standard complement to physical military operations; CISA and FBI have already issued urgent warnings; (4) Commodity supply chains disrupted — Ukraine supplies ~70% of global neon gas (critical for semiconductor chip lithography), is a top-5 global wheat producer, and a significant metals exporter; (5) Risk-off sentiment broadly, with growth/tech under pressure. Our portfolio is positioned for this moment: energy (XOM, CVX, OXY) and cybersecurity (CRWD) are our most direct beneficiaries. We must act on the directional clarity this event provides and redeploy COST capital into higher-conviction geopolitical trades.

new_positions.thesis: Cheniere Energy is the single most direct US beneficiary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine. Europe imports approximately 40% of its natural gas from Russia via pipeline — a supply that is now strategically compromised for years, possibly decades, as European governments accelerate diversification from Russian energy dependence. US LNG exports are Europe's primary substitute: Cheniere operates the Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi terminals, making it the largest US LNG exporter. Post-invasion, European governments will be signing emergency long-term LNG supply contracts at premium prices — this is not hypothetical, it is the only viable path for European energy security. The TTF-to-Henry Hub LNG spread has been at historic levels for months; Russia's invasion has structurally embedded this premium for years. This is not a 3-6 month trade — it is a multi-year structural shift in global energy flows, with Cheniere positioned as the primary infrastructure node of the new energy order. Technical setup is the strongest in the energy complex: RSI=73 (elevated but per Lesson 17, justified in a geopolitical supply shock environment), MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=27 building toward established trend, above SMA50, near upper BB. Per Lesson 17: in a commodity supply shock, chasing strength at RSI=73 is correct — the RSI reset will occur at a much higher price base, not before.

new_positions.thesis: Freeport-McMoRan is a direct commodity beneficiary of the Russia-Ukraine conflict across two distinct vectors. First, Ukraine supplies approximately 70% of global neon gas — a critical input for excimer lasers used in semiconductor chip lithography. A neon supply shock compounds the existing semiconductor shortage and raises input costs across the chip industry, creating commodity repricing tailwinds for materials producers. Second, the broader commodity price surge from war-related supply chain disruption and inflationary sanctions benefits FCX's copper and gold operations: copper is the central metal of the energy transition (each EV requires 4x the copper of ICE; wind and solar installations are copper-intensive) and is denominated and traded globally, meaning supply disruption in one region reprices the global benchmark. Ukraine and Russia together are significant ferrous metal producers; displacement of their supply tightens the entire metals complex. Technical setup is constructive: RSI=59 (not extended), MACD bullish, OBV rising, ADX=20, above SMA50. This is not a Lesson 2 ADX>40 setup — the fundamental Russia/Ukraine catalyst is the primary driver. Inflation/Commodities theme alignment is direct.
new_positions.invalidation: Ceasefire and sanctions removed within 30 days. Copper price falls below $4/lb on global demand destruction. ADX fails to establish above 25 within 4 weeks while OBV turns negative. Neon shortage resolves via alternative supply.
new_positions.timing_note: RSI=59 and MACD bullish provides a technically reasonable entry. The Russia/Ukraine geopolitical catalyst is the primary driver — entering on invasion day captures the commodity repricing that will unfold over coming weeks as markets absorb the full supply implications.

Capital is better deployed into LNG and FCX where Russia/Ukraine provides clear directional momentum with rising OBV and intact trend signals. Accept the -5.2% loss and redeploy.

theme_updates.name: Inflation/Commodities
theme_updates.reason: Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022 creates an extraordinary commodity supply shock layered on top of the existing Fed tightening cycle. Russia and Ukraine together produce ~30% of global wheat, ~70% of global neon gas, significant metals output, and Russia is the world's second-largest oil producer and Europe's largest gas supplier. Sanctions will constrain commodity supply for years. This theme is now the highest-conviction macro call in the portfolio — upgrading to maximum conviction score 5/5.

lessons: Lesson 22 — Geopolitical Supply Shock Override: When a full-scale invasion of a major commodity-producing nation occurs, act on the directional commodity implications immediately and hold existing commodity exposure even when short-term technicals (MACD, OBV) look temporarily weak. Russia's invasion of Ukraine has clear, durable, multi-year implications for energy and commodity prices that override a MACD bearish reading on XOM or OBV flat on CVX. The geopolitical catalyst IS the primary signal; the technical picture will realign within 1-2 weeks as institutional flows respond to the new supply reality. For new entries into commodity beneficiaries (e.g. LNG): justified even at elevated RSI 70-75 when the supply shock is structural and multi-year — per Lesson 17 extended to geopolitical events. Trigger: Full-scale invasion of major commodity producer + existing long exposure = hold with maximum conviction regardless of current MACD. New entries into direct beneficiaries at RSI 70-75 are valid when the supply disruption is multi-year in duration.
